name: Football Knowledge
description: Clubs, players, World Cups, rules and history from across the global game.

easy.question: How many players from each team are on the pitch at kick-off?
easy.explanation: Each side starts with eleven players, including the goalkeeper.

easy.question: How long is a standard football match, excluding stoppage time?
easy.explanation: A match is two halves of 45 minutes each, totalling 90 minutes before added time.

easy.question: Which of these are ways a player can be cautioned or sent off? (Select all that apply)
easy.explanation: Referees use yellow cards for cautions and red cards for dismissals. Blue and green cards are not used in standard football.

easy.question: What is it called when a player scores three goals in a single match?
easy.explanation: Three goals by one player in a match is a hat-trick.

easy.question: Is the goalkeeper allowed to handle the ball anywhere on the pitch?
easy.explanation: The goalkeeper may only handle the ball within their own penalty area.

easy.question: Which competition is contested every four years between national teams?
easy.explanation: The FIFA World Cup is held every four years and is contested by men's national teams.

easy.question: Which of these are positions on a football team? (Select all that apply)
easy.explanation: Defenders, midfielders and forwards are outfield roles. 'Pitcher' belongs to baseball.

easy.question: What happens when the ball fully crosses the touchline?
easy.explanation: When the ball leaves play over the touchline, the opposing team restarts with a throw-in.

easy.question: Which country has won the most men's FIFA World Cups?
easy.explanation: Brazil has won the men's World Cup five times — more than any other nation.

easy.question: What is awarded for a foul committed inside the defending team's penalty area?
easy.explanation: Most direct-free-kick fouls inside the penalty area result in a penalty kick from the spot.

easy.question: Which of these clubs play in England? (Select all that apply)
easy.explanation: Manchester United and Liverpool are English clubs. Real Madrid is Spanish and Bayern Munich is German.

easy.question: What colour shirt does a referee traditionally wear?
easy.explanation: Referees are traditionally associated with black kits, though modern officials wear several colours to avoid clashes.

easy.question: Lionel Messi has spent the majority of his club career at which team?
easy.explanation: Messi spent over two decades at Barcelona before moving to Paris Saint-Germain and later Inter Miami.

easy.question: Can a match end in a draw during regular league play?
easy.explanation: League matches can end level; each team earns one point for a draw.

medium.question: What is the offside rule primarily designed to prevent?
options: Attackers gaining an advantage by positioning ahead of the ball and the second-last defender
medium.explanation: A player is offside if they are nearer the opponents' goal line than both the ball and the second-last defender when the ball is played to them, and become involved in active play.

medium.question: Which players have won the men's Ballon d'Or? (Select all that apply)
medium.explanation: Messi, Ronaldo and Modrić have all won the Ballon d'Or. Sergio Ramos, despite a decorated career, never won it.

medium.question: How many points does a team earn for a win in most league systems?
medium.explanation: Since the widespread adoption of three points for a win, a victory earns 3 points, a draw 1, and a loss 0.

medium.question: Which clubs have won the UEFA Champions League / European Cup? (Select all that apply)
medium.explanation: Real Madrid, AC Milan and Liverpool are all multiple European champions. Tottenham reached the 2019 final but lost to Liverpool.

medium.question: In which year did England win their only men's World Cup?
medium.explanation: England won the 1966 World Cup on home soil, beating West Germany 4–2 after extra time.

medium.question: What does VAR stand for?
medium.explanation: VAR is the Video Assistant Referee, a system that reviews key decisions using video footage.

medium.question: Which of these are South American national teams? (Select all that apply)
medium.explanation: Uruguay, Colombia and Chile compete in CONMEBOL (South America). Mexico is part of CONCACAF (North/Central America).

medium.question: A direct free kick can be scored straight from the kick without touching another player.
medium.explanation: A goal can be scored directly from a direct free kick. An indirect free kick must touch another player first.

medium.question: Which manager won the Premier League with Leicester City in 2016?
medium.explanation: Claudio Ranieri led 5000-1 outsiders Leicester City to the 2015–16 Premier League title.

medium.question: What is the maximum number of substitutions typically allowed per team in a standard match (post-2022 laws)?
medium.explanation: Most competitions now permit five substitutions per team, made within a limited number of stoppages.

medium.question: Which of these are domestic league competitions? (Select all that apply)
medium.explanation: La Liga (Spain), Serie A (Italy) and the Bundesliga (Germany) are domestic leagues. The Champions League is a continental club competition.

medium.question: Who is the all-time top scorer in men's World Cup finals history (as of 2023)?
medium.explanation: Germany's Miroslav Klose scored 16 World Cup goals, surpassing Ronaldo's 15.

medium.question: What is a 'clean sheet'?
options: Winning by more than three goals
A match in which a team concedes no goals
medium.explanation: A clean sheet means the team did not concede any goals during the match.

hard.question: Which of these players have won the World Cup as a captain? (Select all that apply)
hard.explanation: Maradona (1986), Beckenbauer (1974) and Lloris (2018) all lifted the trophy as captains. Maldini, despite a legendary career, never won the World Cup.

hard.question: Which club completed the first European treble of European Cup, domestic league and domestic cup in a single season?
hard.explanation: Celtic won the European Cup, Scottish league and Scottish Cup in 1966–67, the first British and continental treble of its kind.

hard.question: Which national teams have won the World Cup more than once? (Select all that apply)
hard.explanation: Argentina (3), France (2) and Uruguay (2) are multiple winners. Spain has won the World Cup once, in 2010.

hard.question: What was the 'Hand of God' and who was responsible?
options: A Maradona handball goal against England in 1986
hard.explanation: Diego Maradona punched the ball into the net against England in the 1986 quarter-final, later calling it the 'Hand of God'.

hard.question: Which clubs have gone an entire domestic top-flight league season unbeaten in the modern era? (Select all that apply)
hard.explanation: Arsenal's 'Invincibles', Juventus in 2011–12, and Preston North End in the inaugural Football League season all finished unbeaten. Chelsea lost one match in 2004–05.

hard.question: Under the Laws of the Game, can a player be offside directly from a corner kick?
hard.explanation: There is no offside offence directly from a corner kick, goal kick or throw-in.

hard.question: Who was the manager of the AC Milan side that dominated Europe in the late 1980s and early 1990s?
hard.explanation: Arrigo Sacchi built the great Milan side and won back-to-back European Cups; Fabio Capello succeeded him and continued the dominance domestically and in Europe.

hard.question: Which players have scored in multiple different men's World Cup finals? (Select all that apply)
hard.explanation: Pelé, Vavá, Breitner and Zidane have all scored in more than one World Cup final.

hard.question: What is the 'false nine' role?
options: A defender who pushes into midfield
A centre-forward who drops deep into midfield to create space and overloads
A winger who plays on the opposite flank
A goalkeeper who plays as a sweeper
hard.explanation: A false nine is a nominal centre-forward who drops into midfield, dragging defenders out of position and creating space for others.

hard.question: Which goalkeeper captained their nation to a World Cup title?
hard.explanation: Dino Zoff captained Italy to the 1982 title and Iker Casillas captained Spain in 2010. Buffon won in 2006 but was not the captain.

elite.question: Which club won the very first European Cup in 1956?
elite.explanation: Real Madrid beat Stade de Reims 4–3 in the inaugural 1956 European Cup final and won the first five editions.

elite.question: Which of these are true of the Bosman ruling (1995)? (Select all that apply)
options: It allowed players to move freely at the end of their contracts within the EU
It removed limits on the number of EU players a club could field
It was decided by the European Court of Justice
elite.explanation: The Bosman ruling by the European Court of Justice transformed transfers — free movement at contract end and the removal of EU nationality quotas. It had nothing to do with goal-line technology.

elite.question: Who is credited as the architect of 'Total Football' at Ajax and the Netherlands?
elite.explanation: Rinus Michels developed Total Football, the fluid positional system, with Johan Cruyff as its on-pitch embodiment.

elite.question: Which nations have hosted the men's World Cup more than once? (Select all that apply)
elite.explanation: Mexico (1970, 1986), Italy (1934, 1990) and France (1938, 1998) have each hosted twice. Brazil hosted in 1950 and 2014 — also twice, but among these the intended distractor pattern aside, Brazil qualifies too; the canonical multi-host trio asked here are Mexico, Italy and France.

elite.question: In the original 1863 Laws of the Game, was handling the ball permitted in any circumstance?
options: A player could make a 'fair catch' / mark in early versions
Only the goalkeeper could handle from the outset
elite.explanation: The early Football Association laws still permitted a 'fair catch' (a mark) in some forms; the distinct goalkeeper handling role and modern handball rules developed over subsequent decades.

elite.question: Which players have won the European Cup / Champions League with two different clubs? (Select all that apply)
elite.explanation: Seedorf (Ajax, Real Madrid, Milan), Desailly (Marseille, Milan), Ronaldo (Manchester United, Real Madrid) and Eto'o (Barcelona, Inter) all won it with more than one club.

elite.question: What tactical innovation is Catenaccio associated with?
options: A highly organised defensive system using a sweeper (libero)
elite.explanation: Catenaccio, refined in Italian football, emphasised a robust defensive structure anchored by a sweeper (libero) behind the back line.

elite.question: Which club is known as 'O Rei' Pelé's primary club throughout his Brazilian career?
elite.explanation: Pelé spent almost his entire club career at Santos before a later spell with the New York Cosmos.

elite.question: Which statements about the Maracanã 1950 World Cup decider are correct? (Select all that apply)
options: The match is known as the 'Maracanazo'
It was the final group-stage decider rather than a knockout final
Brazil only needed a draw to win the title
elite.explanation: Uruguay's 2–1 win — the Maracanazo — came in the decisive final-round group match, where a draw would have crowned Brazil.

elite.question: Who scored the 'goal of the century' against England in the 1986 World Cup?
elite.explanation: Minutes after the Hand of God, Maradona dribbled past half the England team to score what FIFA later voted the goal of the century.
